root_text.sn52.3:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā anuruddho sāvatthiyaṁ viharati sutanutīre.
root_text.sn52.3:1.2: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yenāyasmā anuruddho tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā anuruddhena saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.sn52.3:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn52.3:1.4: “katamesaṁ āyasmā anuruddho dhammānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto”ti?
root_text.sn52.3:2.1: “Catunnaṁ khvāhaṁ, āvuso, satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto.
root_text.sn52.3:2.3: Idhāhaṁ, āvuso, kāye kāyānupassī viharāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn52.3:2.6: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ—
root_text.sn52.3:2.7: imesaṁ khvāhaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto.
root_text.sn52.3:2.8: Imesañca panāhaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā hīnaṁ dhammaṁ hīnato abbhaññāsiṁ, majjhimaṁ dhammaṁ majjhimato abbhaññāsiṁ, paṇītaṁ dhammaṁ paṇītato abbhaññāsin”ti.

translation_text.sn52.3:0.3: On the Bank of the Sutanu
translation_text.sn52.3:1.1: At one time Venerable Anuruddha was staying near Sāvatthī on the bank of the Sutanu.
translation_text.sn52.3:1.2: Then several mendicants went up to Venerable Anuruddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn52.3:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn52.3:1.4: “What things has Venerable Anuruddha developed and cultivated to attain great direct knowledge?”
translation_text.sn52.3:2.1: “Reverends, I attained great direct knowledge by developing and cultivating the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn52.3:2.3: I meditate observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn52.3:2.4: I meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn52.3:2.6: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn52.3:2.7: I attained great direct knowledge by developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn52.3:2.8: And it was by developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation that I directly knew the lower realm as lower, the middle realm as middle, and the higher realm as higher.”
